steps.title: Set up the earnings equation
steps.content: Let $x$ = hours at job A and $y$ = hours at job B. Since Avery earns \$10 per hour at job A and \$20 per hour at job B, her total earnings are given by: $$10x + 20y = s$$The graph shows all combinations of hours $(x, y)$ that result in earnings of $s$ dollars.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The line intersects the y-axis at the point $(0, 8)$. This means Avery can earn $s$ dollars by working 0 hours at job A and 8 hours at job B.

steps.title: Substitute to find s
steps.content: Substitute $(x, y) = (0, 8)$ into the equation: $$10(0) + 20(8) = s$$ $$160 = s$$That matches choice B — B is correct.
